##NAMES=Katayamalite
##RRUFFID=R120164
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=KLi_3_Ca_7_Ti_2_(SiO_3_)_12_(OH)_2_
##LOCALITY=Iwagi Island, Inland Sea, Ehime Prefecture, Japan
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Donald Doell
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless platy crystals with a brilliant bluish white fluorescence (short wave), associated with albite, wollastonite, pectolite, and aegirine. Crystal used in Andrade et al (2013) study
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R120164
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(K_0.89_Na_0.12_)Li_3.21_(Ca_6.87_Mn_0.04_Ba_0.02_)(Ti_1.79_Zr_0.14_Fe_0.04_Sn_0.02_)(SiO_3_)_12_(OH_1.55_F_0.45_)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 16.9096 b: 9.7287 c: 20.9304 alpha: 90 beta: 112.397 gamma: 90 volume: 3179.5 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
